markdown_content:
## Executive Summary

Manus executed complete yOS v1 build from specifications, achieving 60/60 tests passing across 12 modules with 6549 lines of code. System launched live with API endpoints and dashboard, then transformed into permanent website with 'Cognitive Blueprint' design featuring interactive documentation, API playground, and architectural visualization.


## Context & Intent

User initiated yOS v1 build with simple 'Go' command, then requested testing capabilities and permanent website deployment


## What Was Done

Complete system build (12 modules, 74 files), comprehensive testing suite implementation, Docker containerization, live API deployment, and full website creation with interactive documentation


## Outputs Produced

- [system] yOS v1 Core — Complete cognitive OS with 12 modules, 60 tests, Docker deployment
- [api] Live API Endpoints — 16 endpoints including process, memory, audit, with swagger docs
- [website] yOS Permanent Site — 7-page blueprint-styled site with interactive documentation and API playground
- [dashboard] Management Interface — Live system monitoring and control interface

## Key Decisions & Validations

- Fixed 4 failing tests by improving intent parser heuristics
- Chose 'Cognitive Blueprint' design theme for website (navy blue, IBM Plex fonts, architectural aesthetic)
- Implemented architecture visualization as site navigation
- Used simulation mode for initial deployment pending LiteLLM configuration

## Lessons Learned

Worked well:

- Complete autonomous build execution
- Comprehensive test coverage achieving 100% pass rate
- Live deployment with immediate testing capability
- Design-driven website development
Failed / suboptimal:

- GitHub PAT lacked repo permissions for direct push
- Initial 4 test failures due to simplistic intent parser
Discoveries:

- yOS architecture translates well to interactive website navigation
- Blueprint aesthetic effectively communicates technical sophistication

## Challenges & Blockers

- GitHub permissions preventing direct repository push
- Need for LiteLLM configuration for full API functionality

## Open Questions

- Should upgrade to web-db-user for live backend integration?
- Add custom domain (yos.space) for production deployment?
- Implement interactive routing visualization page?

## Next Steps

- Configure GitHub PAT with repo permissions or manual push
- Set up LiteLLM integration for full API functionality
- Consider domain registration and production deployment
- Evaluate adding interactive routing rules visualization
